corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

top_candidates.sentence: SNNs significantly outperform all competing networks in pairwise comparisons (paired Wilcoxon test across datasets) as reported in Table (left panel).
On 75 small datasets with less than 1000 data points, random forests and SVMs outperform SNNs and other FNNs.
Overall, SNNs have outperformed state of the art machine learning methods on UCI datasets with more than 1,000 data points.
87 A4.3 Tox21 challenge data set: Hyperparameters . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .
Hyperparameters such as number of layers (blocks), neurons per layer, learning rate, and dropout rate, are adjusted by grid-search for each dataset on a separate validation set (see Section A4).
The benchmark comprises 121 classification datasets from the UCI Machine Learning repository from diverse application areas, such as physics, geology, or biology.
The selected hyperparameters served to evaluate the methods in terms of accuracy on the pre-defined test sets (details on the hyperparameter selection are given in Section A4).
We ranked the methods by their accuracy for each prediction task and compared their average ranks.
FNNs that perform well are typically shallow and, therefore cannot exploit many levels of abstract representations.
SNNs significantly outperformed all competing FNN methods at 121 UCI tasks, outperformed all competing methods at the Tox21 dataset, and set a new record at an astronomy data set.

Recurrent neural networks (RNNs) achieved new levels at speech and natural language 31st Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NIPS 2017), Long Beach, CA, USA. processing, for example at the TIMIT benchmark or at language translation , and are already employed in mobile devices .
For example, the HIGGS challenge, the Merck Molecular Activity challenge, and the Tox21 Data challenge were all won by FNNs with at most four hidden layers.
Each compared FNN method was optimized with respect to its architecture and hyperparameters on a validation set that was then removed from the subsequent analysis.
Therefore, SNNs work well for architectures with many layers, allowed us to introduce a novel regularization scheme, and learn very robustly.

On 121 UCI benchmark datasets, SNNs have outperformed other FNNs with and without normalization techniques, such as batch, layer, and weight normalization, or specialized architectures, such as Highway or Residual networks.
We introduce self-normalizing neural networks (SNNs) to enable high-level abstract representations.
